title: hotato corpus contribution label, v1
description: The label that accompanies one contributed dual-channel recording in the hotato open test corpus. It extends the bundled scenario shape (src/hotato/data/scenarios/*.json) with the provenance, consent, PII, and attestation fields a REAL recording must carry. The turn-taking ground truth reuses the scenario fields verbatim: category, caller_onset_sec, expected, and an optional reference_render with human-labelled segment timings. Governing policy: docs/CORPUS-GOVERNANCE.md. Nothing here implies an accuracy percentage; validity is reported as millisecond error + a confusion matrix (see docs/BENCHMARK.md).

id.description: Stable slug for the contribution, e.g. 'call-042-warranty-interrupt'.

category.description: The human label scored by the did_yield confusion matrix.

source_type.description: HONESTY FIELD. 'real-call' = an actual call. 'role-played' = actors reading a script (real acoustics, no real customer). 'synthetic' = rendered audio; a synthetic clip is NEVER labelled real and is only in the corpus as an explicit, self-declared example.

audio.description: Filename of the dual-channel WAV, resolved next to this label file unless a path is passed to the validator.

sample_rate.description: Hz, e.g. 8000 for telephony, 16000 for wideband.

caller_onset_sec.description: Human-labelled moment the caller takes (or attempts) the floor. Required.

reference_render.description: Human-labelled ground-truth timings, in seconds. For a real recording these are hand-labelled, not rendered. Supply only the ones you can defend; the benchmark harness reports error for exactly the signals you provide and leaves the rest blank.

transcript.description: Reader context only. Never consumed by the scorer, never used as a label. Must itself be free of PII.

license.description: The corpus is redistributed under MIT. A clip that cannot be released under MIT with consent does not enter the corpus.

consent.description: Required for real-call and role-played sources with identifiable parties. See docs/CORPUS-GOVERNANCE.md for the release template.

method.description: How PII was kept out. Redaction must preserve timing (same-duration tone/silence), never cut samples.

phi_free.description: Must be true. PHI is out of scope regardless of consent.

attestation.description: The contributor's attestation, part of the merge record (docs/CORPUS-GOVERNANCE.md, Data-handling). For synthetic/role-played-no-real-people sources, consent_on_file may be true vacuously, but pii_removed, no_phi, and right_to_release_mit must hold.
